title: lesquerella engelmannii subsp. alba
intro: Lesquerella engelmannii subsp. alba, also known as Engelmann's bladderpod or white bladderpod, is a plant species belonging to the mustard family (Brassicaceae). This plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly in its seeds, which are used to produce a compound called lesquerella, a fatty acid with potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The seeds of Lesquerella engelmannii subsp. alba have been used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and infections, due to their antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. Lesquerella seeds can be used in herbal preparations, such as tinctures, infusions, and salves, to harness their medicinal benefits and promote wound healing, skin health, and overall well-being.
